chapter.label: Tale 90
chapter.title: Tbe death of two anchorites mountain to the sea, and there be found a ship riding at anchor. He

paragraphs.text: ON MOUNT PTERGION asked the shipmaster and the crew to come up into the mountain with him and bury the brother. They agreed willingly and, taking up There were two anchorites beyond Rossos, living on Mount the necessary tools, went up with him. They dug <a grave> and Prergion <= the little wing> near the River Piapi and the monastery buried the brother's body. One of the sailors, whose name was of Abba Theodosios at Skopelos. The elder <anchorite> died and ‘Thalilaios, very impressed with the virtue of the elder, asked him if his disciple offered a prayer and buried him on the mountain. A few he could stay with him. The elder told him that he would not be days later the disciple of the anchorite came down from the able to support the rigour of the ascetic life. The younger man mountain and approached the inhabited world <oikoumené>. He replied that he was sure he could endure it. So he remained with the came across a man working his land and said to him: ‘Of your elder and was there for a whole year, making a great effort in charity good fellow, take your mattock and spade and come with ascetic endeavour. When the year was up, brother Thalilaios me’. The peasant did what the anchorite requested at once. When prostrated himself before the elder and said: ‘Pray for me father, they came up into the mountain the anchorite showed the man who for, thanks to your prayers, God has relieved me of suffering and lived in the world the tomb of his elder, <i.e> the grave of the I am no longer afflicted by discomfort, nor does this intemperate anchorite and said to him: ‘Dig here’. Whilst the peasant was weather trouble me. 1 neither faint with the heat nor shiver with the digging, the anchorite stood in prayer. When his prayer was cold; T am in great comfort’. The elder blessed him and brother finished, he embraced the man from the world saying: ‘Brother, pray Thelalaios remained with him for two and a ball years; thea he for me, sir’. He went down into the grave, placed himself on top of perceived that his end was near. He begged the elder: ‘Take me to the elder and surrendered his soul. The man from the world filled Jerusalem so I can venerate the Holy Cross and the Holy Sepulchre the grave in and gave thanks to God. When he had gone about a of Christ our God; for these are the days in which the Lord will stone’s throw down the mountain-side he said to himself: ‘I really take me to himself". The elder therefore took him and went to the

They worshipped at the holy and venerable places, then sack-cloth and on his shoulders he had an over-garment made of they went down to the holy Jordan and were baptised. Three days rushes. In a gentle voice he said to me: ‘Tell me, Abba George, did later, brother Thalilaios died. The elder buried him in the Copratha it really seem just to you, sir, that after so many labours and so Javra. Some time later, Abba George the anchorite himself departed much endurance, I should be left outside the church you are <this life> and the fathers of the same lavra at Copratha buried him building? Out of respect for the worth of the elder, I said to him: in their own church. ‘Who in fact are you, sic”? He said: 'I am Peter the grazer of the 1 holy Jordan.” I arose at dawn and enlarged the plan of the church.
